prerequisites: A commercetools project, identified by its project_key (the unique identifier for your top-level commercetools data container).
An API Client created in the commercetools Merchant Center (or via the API Clients endpoint), which yields the client_id and client_secret. Grant it read-level scopes for the data you intend to sync.

The region your project runs in, used to determine the correct API and authorization host (for example us-central1.gcp, us-east-2.aws, europe-west1.gcp, eu-central-1.aws, or australia-southeast1.gcp). Regions are fully isolated from one another.
The host (cloud provider) for your region, since commercetools regions run on either Google Cloud (gcp) or AWS (aws).
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials
authMethods.summary: commercetools authorizes HTTP API requests using OAuth 2.0. The connector authenticates with the client_id and client_secret of an API Client via the client credentials grant: it POSTs grant_type=client_credentials to the region's auth token endpoint (https://auth.<region>.commercetools.com/oauth/token) with the credentials sent as HTTP Basic auth, receives a Bearer access token, and sends it in the Authorization header of subsequent API calls. Map the client_id and client_secret secret fields to this method.

setupSteps.title: Create an API Client in commercetools
setupSteps.body: In the commercetools Merchant Center, open your project and create an API Client (Settings > Developer settings > API clients), or use the API Clients API endpoint. Assign read-level scopes covering the resources you want to extract. On creation, commercetools shows the client_id and client_secret once, so copy them immediately.

setupSteps.title: Note your project_key, region, and host
setupSteps.body: Record the project_key that uniquely identifies your project. Determine your region (for example us-central1.gcp, us-east-2.aws, europe-west1.gcp, eu-central-1.aws, or australia-southeast1.gcp) and the host (the cloud provider, gcp or aws). These together select the correct API and authorization endpoints, since commercetools regions are isolated from one another.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter client_id and client_secret from the API Client, plus project_key, region, and host. Set start_date to the earliest date from which to sync data. The connector uses these values to request an OAuth access token from the region's token endpoint and then call the commercetools HTTP API for your project.
